title: Why Starbucks Is Betting On Employees As Creators
tldr: Starbucks is turning employees into content creators to humanize the brand — a live example of employee-generated content at enterprise scale.
angle: Break down what Starbucks' employee-creator program teaches about scaling authentic EGC without losing governance.

title: Human Stories, Real Impact: Employee Storytelling is Shaping Brands in the Age of AI
tldr: Argues employee storytelling is becoming the antidote to AI-generated sameness, giving brands human credibility.
angle: Make the case for employee storytelling as a strategic differentiator when AI floods every channel.

title: Half of Britons have seen AI-generated actors in ads – and they don't like them
tldr: New YouGov data shows widespread consumer awareness of and distaste for AI actors in advertising — a signal about the limits of synthetic media.
angle: Use the YouGov numbers to argue why human-centered content beats synthetic media on trust.

title: How Deutsche Welle Builds Audience Strategy Across 30-Plus Languages in the Age of Algorithmic Discovery
tldr: DW is embedding audience development inside the newsroom and rethinking distribution as algorithmic discovery replaces the old search-and-social playbook.
angle: Explore what a multilingual, newsroom-embedded audience strategy means for brand publishers facing AI discovery.

title: Brand-as-creator strategy: what B2B marketers can learn from Ryanair, Wendy's, and Duolingo
tldr: Breaks down how consumer brands built creator-style social personas and what B2B can borrow — with concrete campaign mechanics.
angle: Adapt the brand-as-creator playbook for cautious B2B comms teams — where it works and where it flops.

title: Why a once-anonymous creator unmasked herself to build a bigger media brand
tldr: Kristi Cook's YouTube channel exploded once she revealed her face — a case study in how human presence drives audience connection.
angle: Unpack why showing a human face outperforms polished anonymity in the attention economy.

title: 4 lessons from Oatly on building content that powers campaigns
tldr: Oatly's look book shows how one editorial content asset can keep fueling campaigns long after launch — a repurposing and content-lifecycle case study.
why_fresh: solid brand case study, familiar concept
angle: Show how a single anchor content asset can be architected to power an entire campaign cycle.

title: Business Insider's big bet against aggregation
tldr: BI is restructuring reporters to "command their beat" doing everything from features to spot news — a bet on depth over aggregation in the AI era.
angle: What BI's anti-aggregation editorial operating model signals for content teams rethinking roles.

title: 3 trust-building tactics for AI communications
tldr: As AI adoption grows, audiences want to know how and why organizations use it and where humans still fit — practical guidance for communicators.
angle: Draft a communicator's playbook for talking about AI honestly, keeping humans visible.

title: AI Is Telling Every Brand's Story. Here's How Companies Are Shaping It
tldr: Explores how brands are trying to influence how AI answer engines describe them — the emerging discipline of AI narrative control.
angle: How to manage your corporate narrative when LLMs, not you, are the primary storyteller.

title: Media Briefing: AI visibility is becoming publishers' newest currency
tldr: Publishers are treating prominence in AI answer engines as a new must-have metric and selling it to advertisers.
angle: Why 'AI visibility' may become as important as SEO — and how content teams should measure it.

title: Turning Workers Into Influencers? Key Employment Issues for Employers Before Starting
tldr: Legal look at the risks of employee-influencer programs — disclosure, IP, liability — the governance side of EGC that most guides skip.
angle: Pair this with the Starbucks story: the governance guardrails every employee-creator program needs.

title: The Scoop: Malört made its bad flavor part of the brand experience
tldr: Malört leaned into brutal customer descriptions ('fermented back sweat') as its selling point — a case study in anti-polish, human-honest branding.
angle: When embracing your flaws beats polish: the audience psychology of honest, self-deprecating brand voice.

title: Что делать, когда первый восторг от вайбкодинга прошел, а ошибки остались?
tldr: Seven real automation scenarios and the main pitfalls of AI-assisted coding — practical for content ops teams building low-code workflows.
angle: Translate the 'vibecoding hangover' into lessons for content teams automating routine work.

title: The AI-Driven Content Strategy: How (and When) to Use AI for Content Marketing
tldr: Orbit Media lays out a framework for where AI helps content marketing and where it hurts — with a clear when-not-to lens.
angle: Build your own decision matrix for when AI belongs in the content workflow — and when humans must stay.
